description_long: This object consists of five irregularly shaped stone blocks arranged in a compact, pyramid-like stack. The stones have a weathered, rough surface with visible patches of green moss and lichen, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. Each block is slightly rounded at the edges and corners, with subtle variations in size and shape, giving the structure a natural, unrefined appearance. The coloration is predominantly gray with hints of brown and green, and the stones appear dense and solid. The overall form is stable, with three blocks forming the base, two in the middle, and one on top. The surface texture is uneven, with small pits and crevices visible across the stones.
description: A stack of five weathered, mossy stone blocks arranged in a pyramid shape, featuring rough, irregular surfaces and natural coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full pyramid arrangement of five mossy, gray stone blocks with visible surface texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth and thickness of the stacked blocks, emphasizing their rounded edges and vertical alignment.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the stacked arrangement and surface details of the stone blocks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the vertical stack and the irregular, weathered surfaces of the stones.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or garden retaining wall
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss and lichen growth on surfaces and edges, with some surface pitting and discoloration indicating long-term outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: stacked in a compact, interlocking arrangement
